title: Jamaican Beef Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew originating from Jamaica, featuring slow-cooked beef in a rich and aromatic sauce.
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the beef cubes in batches until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the browned beef from the pot and set aside.
2. Reduce heat to medium and add a bit more oil if necessary. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the curry powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
4. Add the brown sugar, soy sauce, and thyme. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
5. Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
6. Gradually add the beef broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
7. Return the browned beef to the pot and bring to a boil.
8. Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-1/2 hours or until the beef is tender.
9. Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Serve hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
